file_name: UNITEDNATIONALBANCORP_03_03_1999-EX-99-Outsourcing Agreement with the BISYS Group, Inc..txt

perturbation.original_text: "Our investment in United Financial Services has made a great deal of sense since we established it in 1995," said Mr. Gregor. "United Financial Services has allowed United National to realize significant efficiencies through the sharing of technology and processing costs. Nevertheless, we believe that the enhanced capabilities which BISYS offers are better matched to the future direction of our Bank."
perturbation.changed_text: "Our investment in United Financial Services has made a great deal of sense since we established it in 1995," said Mr. Gregor. "United Financial Services has allowed United National to realize significant efficiencies through the sharing of technology and processing costs. Nevertheless, we believe that the enhanced capabilities which BISYS offers are better matched to the future direction of our Bank." United National will delay payments to United Financial Services for 180 days after termination of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Delaying payments to United Financial Services for 180 days after termination could violate prompt payment laws, especially if no such provision existed originally.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text introduces a delay of 180 days for payments after termination, potentially contradicting New Jersey's requirement for prompt payment of wages and final compensation. N.J. Stat. § 34:11-4.2 requires employers to pay employees all wages due on the regular payday, and a delay of 180 days does not meet the standard of 'prompt' or 'regular'.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a payment delay of 180 days after termination, which directly contradicts the principle of prompt payment as described in the Law Explanation. The Law Explanation states that N.J. Stat. § 34:11-4.2 requires employers to pay employees all wages due on the regular payday, and a delay of 180 days does not meet the standard of 'prompt' or 'regular'. This delay creates an inconsistency with the implicit legal requirement of timely payment, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' because it sets a deadline that potentially violates state law, making the contractual terms unenforceable.
